title: Development and Validation of a Sepsis Mortality Risk Score for Sepsis-3 Patients in Intensive Care Unit

sample_size.source_quote: For model development, we enrolled a total of 5,443 patients fulfilling the sepsis-3 criteria.

mortality_predictors.cutoff_value: Low: 0–6 points (3.2% mortality); Moderate: 7–11 points (12.4%); High: 12–14 points (30.7%); Very high: ≥15 points (68.1%)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Race, age, need for mechanical ventilation, lactate, temperature, SBP, SpO2, BUN, WBC, Ca, HR, RR, INR
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The SMRS had good discrimination (AUC: 0.789) in the development set, which was better than other severity scores (Figure 2A)... SMRS was well-discriminated in the external validation set (AUC: 0.765)
In our study, for predicting 30-day mortality, the SOFA score had a low discriminatory power (AUC: 0.69).
SMRS was well-discriminated in the external validation set (AUC: 0.765), which was greater than APACHE IV and SAPS II (AUC: APACHE IV 0.754, SAPS II 0.751
SMRS was well-discriminated in the external validation set (AUC: 0.765), which was greater than APACHE IV and SAPS II (AUC: APACHE IV 0.754, SAPS II 0.751

mortality_predictors.effect_size: Two-fold increase in odds of death
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Race, age, lactate, temperature, SBP, SpO2, BUN, WBC, Ca, HR, RR, INR
mortality_predictors.source_quote: A two-fold increase in the odds of death was observed in our model in patients requiring mechanical ventilation within the first 24 h of admission.
